meaning: adj.竞赛的,论争的,[心]动机争胜性的
examples: It is a kind of person rides to multiply horses or control the agonistic project of carriage.
They now have the antagonistic and agonistic muscles working against each other, and this hurts their speed.
Now coauthor Amanda Lea, also of UCLA, suggests, "We need to rethink our traditional view of affliative interactions as good and agonistic ones as bad."
